sections.subtitle: Recent Form
sections.text: Girona FC have shown mixed results in their last 5 matches, securing 2 wins and suffering 3 losses, with a notable defensive struggle as they’ve conceded in all 7 recent games. Osasuna, on the other hand, are in better shape with an unbeaten streak of 3 matches (2 wins, 1 draw), displaying solid attacking intent, especially late in games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these teams, results have been split, with Girona winning 4-0 at home and Osasuna edging a 2-1 victory at their ground. Matches between them often see over 2.5 goals (4 of the last 5) and both teams scoring, indicating a competitive and open game.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Girona, sitting 17th with 17 goals scored and 34 conceded, struggle defensively (worst in the league) but have overperformed their xG. Osasuna, 13th with 18 goals scored and 21 conceded, show more balance and efficiency, particularly in creating chances late in matches.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: Girona’s Cristhian Stuani (4 goals in last 5) is in fine form, while Viktor Tsygankov adds creativity. For Osasuna, Ante Budimir (6 goals this season) and Rubén García (2 goals, 2 assists recently) are major threats.

sections.subtitle: Tactical Outlook
sections.text: Girona will likely push for goals at home but must address defensive lapses, often conceding first. Osasuna’s ability to score late and their physicality in duels (especially aerially) could exploit Girona’s frailties.

sections.subtitle: Prediction
sections.text: Given Osasuna’s current form and Girona’s defensive issues, the away side holds a slight edge as per Sofascore’s probabilities (42% win chance). Expect a tight match, potentially with both teams scoring, but Osasuna may clinch it late.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At the break, Girona FC leads Osasuna 1-0 at Estadi Montilivi. The home side secured the advantage with a late first-half goal from Vladyslav Vanat in the 44th minute, capitalizing on a well-worked opportunity. Girona has shown a slight edge in possession and created a big chance, which they converted. Osasuna, despite not registering a shot on target, has shown promise in their attacking intent with numerous penalty area entries, suggesting they remain a threat despite the scoreline.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Girona started brightly with strong attacking spells early on, dominating the initial minutes. Osasuna responded with a sustained period of pressure in the middle of the half, building momentum and pushing forward aggressively. However, Girona regained control intermittently with notable attacking surges, particularly towards the end of the half, culminating in their goal. Osasuna had their moments late in the half with intense forward pushes, but they couldn’t convert their momentum into a tangible result.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key performers
halftimeSections.text: For Girona, Vladyslav Vanat has been pivotal, not only scoring the opener but also posing a constant threat up front. Álex Moreno has also stood out with a crucial assist and a high rating, showcasing his influence in creating opportunities. On the defensive end, Daley Blind has been solid with key tackles and clearances. For Osasuna, Javi Galán has been their standout player, creating chances with several key passes. Ante Budimir, despite not scoring, remains a focal point in attack and will be crucial if Osasuna are to turn things around.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical insights
halftimeSections.text: Girona has effectively utilized their possession to penetrate the final third, evidenced by a higher number of entries compared to Osasuna. Their ability to win duels has also helped them maintain control in key moments. Osasuna, on the other hand, has shown resilience in aerial battles and has entered the penalty area frequently, hinting at a direct approach. However, their inability to create big chances or test the goalkeeper suggests a lack of precision in the final third. Adjustments in their attacking structure will be vital in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: Girona will likely look to protect their lead while exploiting counter-attacking opportunities, with Vanat remaining their key outlet. Osasuna, trailing, will need to push for an equalizer, potentially opening up spaces at the back. Their expected goals indicate they’ve had the potential to score, so converting entries into shots on target will be their focus. Given both teams’ attacking intent in the first half, the second half could see more goalmouth action as Osasuna chases the game and Girona looks to capitalize on any defensive lapses.
